name: North Brabant (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: from the historic Brabant chequy livery
from the historic Brabant chequy livery

description: A chequy field of 24 red and white squares (eight columns by three rows), with red squares in the upper-hoist and lower-fly corners.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 21 January 1959. The pattern reproduces the Brabantse bonte vlag, the medieval livery of the Dukes of Brabant.

features.symbolism: the red-and-white chequy pattern is the historic livery of the Dukes of Brabant, derived from the arms of the Counts of Leuven who became Dukes of Brabant in the 12th century
